name: riemann-20-case-fixture-benchmark
description: Balanced 20-case theorem fixture set for CLI and proof-verification evaluation.

cases.rationale: Canonical right-identity of addition on naturals.
Canonical left-identity of addition on naturals.
Simple multiplicative identity.
Simple multiplicative identity on the left.
Standard subtraction simplification on naturals.

cases.statement: forall a b c : Nat, a + (b + c) = (a + b) + c
cases.rationale: Associativity is a basic algebraic rewrite.
Direct implication introduction and reuse.

cases.statement: forall P Q : Prop, P -> Q -> P /\ Q
cases.rationale: Straightforward conjunction construction.

cases.statement: forall P Q : Prop, P /\ Q -> Q /\ P
cases.rationale: Commutativity of conjunction by unpacking assumptions.

cases.statement: forall P Q : Prop, P -> Q -> P \/ Q
cases.rationale: Immediate disjunction introduction.
False for all naturals except no cases; impossible as stated.
Strict inequality is irreflexive on naturals.
Only true for zero, so the universal claim is false.

cases.statement: forall a b : Nat, a + b = a
cases.rationale: Fails whenever b is nonzero.

cases.statement: forall a b : Nat, a * b = 0
cases.rationale: Fails for positive inputs such as a = 1 and b = 1.

cases.statement: forall P Q : Prop, (P -> Q) -> P -> ~Q
cases.rationale: Contradicts the assumption that P implies Q.
Useful expected pipeline failure without explicit classical handling.

cases.statement: forall P Q : Prop, P -> ~P -> Q
cases.rationale: Useful expected pipeline failure even though the theorem is provable by explosion.
No natural number satisfies this equality.

cases.statement: forall a b : Nat, a = b
cases.rationale: The universal equality is false because distinct naturals need not be equal.
